Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for NorthWind using search_emails (query: "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski"). Find Pam's formal escalation to David (Apr 24) with 4 demands and end-of-Friday deadline, Catalina's Apr 14 recovery promise that was never delivered, and Pam's original early-April Denver relocation requirements email (~Apr 1).
OE 2: Search Slack for NorthWind using conversations_search_messages (query: "NorthWind" or "Raj Patel" or "Emilia"). Find David's save-or-lose declaration, Chloe's triage, and Blessing's admission about the piano turn radius.
OE 3: Search CRM for NorthWind using crm_search_companies or crm_search_deals (query: "NorthWind"). Find deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 ($14,700) and the NorthWind Denver recovery context engagement note (linked to company_northwind).
OE 3b: List CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements to discover Julian's inbound complaint call from Emilia Cruz about piano damage (Apr 12). This engagement has "No owner assigned" and is NOT linked to any company (company_ids is empty), so it will not appear in a NorthWind-filtered engagement query — the agent must browse engagements broadly or find it referenced in other sources (e.g., David's internal retention email or Slack).
OE 4: Search emails for Marcus's concession figures using search_emails (query: "NorthWind concession" or "Marcus concession"). Find internal email from marcus.thorne@moveops.com to david.chen@moveops.com with piano repair ~$3,500, service credit 10% of $14,700 = $1,470, total ~$4,970, requires Elena's approval.
OE 5: Search QuickBooks for NorthWind using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: customer name "NorthWind"). Confirm current billing status and $14,700 contract value.
OE 6: Look up Pam Kowalski's contact information using crm_search_contacts (query: "Pam Kowalski"). Pam is not in the Contacts service but exists as a CRM contact. Confirm pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com.
OE 7: Look up David Chen's email using contacts_search_contacts (query: "David"). Confirm david.chen@moveops.com.
OE 8: Search Airtable for NorthWind relocation records using airtable_search_records (query: "NorthWind" or "Raj Patel" or "Emilia Cruz"). Find Raj Patel's record showing office server move with chain-of-custody breach, and Emilia Cruz's record showing piano damage during residential move — both with coordinator and status details.
OE 9: Search Linear for existing NorthWind issues using linear_search_issues (query: "NorthWind"). Check whether any tracking tickets already exist for NorthWind to avoid creating duplicates and to understand what's already being tracked.
OE 10: Update NorthWind CRM record using crm_create_engagement — logging current account status: Raj chain-of-custody breach, Emilia piano damage unresolved, Catalina's broken Apr 14 promise, Julian's undelivered Apr 12 callback.
OE 11: Create Linear tracking ticket using linear_create_issue — title relating to NorthWind retention, description covering all 4 open items from Pam's demands, urgent priority.
OE 12: Send email to Pam Kowalski (pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com) using send_email — covering each of Pam's 4 demands, flagging items needing David or Elena sign-off, referencing concession package.
OE 13: Send summary email to David Chen (david.chen@moveops.com) using send_email — summarizing what was sent to Pam, financial commitments made, and what still needs his approval.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 A CRM engagement was created (via crm_create_engagement or similar CRM write tool) for NorthWind Technologies reflecting the current account recovery status.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called crm_create_engagement with company_ids ['company_northwind'] and received a successful response with engagement ID '514048df0fef44778bdaa73a058aa955'. The engagement note documents the full April incident history and current escalation status.
✓Run #2The trajectory confirms crm_create_engagement was called, creating a detailed engagement note titled 'NorthWind Account — Critical Escalation Status' for company_northwind. The tool returned successfully.
✓Run #3The agent called crm_create_engagement with company_ids=['company_northwind'] and contact_ids=['contact_pam_kowalski'], including a comprehensive note covering both incidents, broken commitments, concession posture, and all open items. The tool returned an output file (tool_call_result_16.json), indicating success.
✓Run #4The agent called crm_create_engagement with company_ids=['company_northwind'] and a comprehensive body reflecting the current account recovery/crisis status. The tool returned a successful email_id result.
✓Run #5The agent called crm_create_engagement with company_ids=['company_northwind'] and a comprehensive body covering incident history, concession analysis, and current account status. The tool returned a successful result.
✓Run #6The agent called crm_create_engagement with company_ids=['company_northwind'] and contact_ids=['contact_pam_kowalski'], creating a comprehensive engagement note. The tool returned a result file indicating success.
2 A Linear issue was created (via linear_create_issue) as an urgent tracking ticket for the NorthWind account retention or recovery.
outcome

✓Run #3The agent called linear_create_issue with title 'NorthWind retention: remediation tracking — all open items', referencing NorthWind, Raj Patel, Emilia Cruz, retention, and all open items. The tool returned an output file (tool_call_result_19.json), indicating success.

✓Run #6The agent called linear_create_issue with title 'NorthWind retention — open remediation items tracker (Pam deadline Apr 26)' and detailed description referencing NorthWind account. The tool returned a result file indicating success.

5 The email to [pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com](mailto:pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com) addresses the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach — including acknowledgment that equipment was loaded before the IT escort arrived and/or placed in unauthorized storage.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body contains a full section titled 'RAJ PATEL — CHAIN-OF-CUSTODY INCIDENT' explicitly describing how SentinelChain loaded servers before the IT escort arrived and placed equipment in unauthorized overnight warehouse storage.
✓Run #2The trajectory and final response confirm the email to Pam addressed the Raj Patel server move incident including the chain-of-custody breach, crew arriving before IT escort, and unauthorized warehouse storage. The CRM note and email both explicitly covered this.
✓Run #3The email to Pam explicitly includes the full chain-of-custody breach timeline: 7:00 AM crew arrival, 9:00 AM IT escort, unauthorized loading, SentinelChain South San Francisco warehouse overnight storage, and all three of Victor's violated requirements.
✓Run #4The email to Pam explicitly addresses the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach, including the crew loading at 7 AM before the 9 AM IT escort arrived, the equipment being routed to overnight warehouse storage, and the missing chain-of-custody log.
✓Run #5The email to Pam explicitly addresses the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach, including that the SentinelChain crew arrived at 7:00 AM before the 9:00 AM IT escort, equipment was loaded without authorization, and placed into SentinelChain's warehouse for overnight storage.
✓Run #6The email to Pam's email address extensively covers the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach, explicitly mentioning the 7 AM early loading before the 9 AM escort arrival and the overnight warehouse storage in South San Francisco.
6 The email to [pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com](mailto:pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com) addresses Emilia Cruz's piano damage – including acknowledgment of the damage and a resolution path or next steps.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body has a dedicated section 'EMILIA CRUZ — PIANO DAMAGE CLAIM' acknowledging the scratch, the missed callback, and proposing MoveOps cover the full $3,500 repair cost as the resolution path.
✓Run #2The trajectory confirms the email to Pam addressed the Emilia Cruz piano damage, stating MoveOps owns the claim and Emilia won't pay the $1,200, with next steps provided.
✓Run #3The email to Pam explicitly addresses Emilia Cruz's Steinway being scratched, the dropped callback, the $1,200 KeyMove rider being absorbed, the $3,500 repair cost, and a commitment to a reimbursement proposal within 48 hours.
✓Run #4The email to Pam explicitly addresses Emilia Cruz's piano damage — acknowledging the scratch during stairwell extraction, the missed callback on April 12, the $1,200 insurance rider, the $3,500 estimated repair cost, and a resolution path committing to a dollar figure by Monday.
✓Run #5The email to Pam explicitly addresses Emilia Cruz's piano damage — acknowledging the scratch during extraction, the missed callback, and committing to MoveOps absorbing the $1,200 claim plus personal ownership of coordinating the resolution.
✓Run #6The email to Pam addresses Emilia Cruz's piano damage with acknowledgment of the Steinway scratch, the missed callback, the $1,200 insurance rider, ~$3,500 repair estimate, and a specific resolution path with Julian personally owning the claim coordination.
7 The email to [pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com](mailto:pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com) or the summary to [david.chen@moveops.com](mailto:david.chen@moveops.com) references a commercial concession, service credit, or compensation amount related to the NorthWind account failures (e.g., approximately $1,470, 10% credit, $3,500 repair coverage, or similar).
outcome
 ✓✗✓✗✓✗ 50%
✓Run #1The email to Pam references the $3,500 repair cost ('MoveOps will cover the full $3,500 repair cost directly') as a specific commercial concession/compensation amount tied to the service failure.
✗Run #2The agent explicitly stated it did NOT quote a concession number in the email to Pam ('Did NOT quote a number — told Pam it's coming by Wednesday'). The email to David also does not appear to include specific dollar amounts (~$1,470 or ~$3,500). Marcus's concession package was never finalized, and QuickBooks returned no NorthWind invoices to derive the 10% figure from.
✓Run #3The email to Pam mentions absorbing the $1,200 KeyMove rider and the $3,500 estimated piano repair cost. The email to David also references the $1,200 rider and $3,500 repair cost explicitly. While $1,470 (10% of $14,700) is not specifically cited, the criterion allows 'or similar' and the $3,500 repair coverage and $1,200 rider are both referenced.
✗Run #4The email to Pam explicitly states 'I am not going to send you a number that has not been properly approved internally' and does NOT include a specific commercial concession figure. The David Slack message also notes no finalized numbers. While the David message mentions ~$3,500 and the $1,200 rider, no approved concession number was included in either communication as a formal proposal.
✓Run #5The email to Pam mentions the $1,200 piano damage cost absorbed by MoveOps, and the David summary references the $14,700 total invoice, $3K goodwill credit, and the tiered discount model with 10% (25.6% margin). Commercial concession figures are included in both communications.
✗Run #6The agent did not include a specific commercial concession amount in the email to Pam or the summary to David. The agent explicitly stated it did not commit to any numbers and that David needs to finalize the concession package. No specific figures like $1,470, 10% credit, or $3,500 repair coverage were committed to in either communication.
8 The email to [pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com](mailto:pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com) flags that certain commitments require David or Elena's approval before being finalized (or similar language indicating sign-off is needed).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email explicitly flags multiple items with '⚠️ NEEDS DAVID/ELENA SIGN-OFF' for the incident response document, the $3,500 piano reimbursement (needs David), the named account lead (needs Elena), and the concession numbers (needs David + Elena).
✓Run #2The final response confirms that the email to Pam flagged that the $1,200 piano absorption mechanism needs David/Marcus sign-off and the commercial concession needs Elena sign-off. The criterion is met.
✓Run #3The email to Pam explicitly flags multiple items requiring David or Elena's sign-off: the final incident report requires David's sign-off, the exact repair reimbursement structure requires David/Elena sign-off, the named coordinator requires Elena's staffing decision, and the commercial concession package requires David's approval.
✓Run #4The email to Pam explicitly flags multiple times that items require David Chen's and Elena Rostova's sign-off — including the SentinelChain vendor consequences, the piano repair commitment, permanent AM assignment, and the commercial concession package.
✓Run #5The email to Pam explicitly flags with ⚠️ markers three items requiring David/Elena sign-off: formal incident response to Victor, named account owner decision, and the specific commercial concession number. The language is clear that these require authorization.
✓Run #6The email to Pam repeatedly flags items needing David/Elena sign-off using '⚠️ Needs David/Elena sign-off:' markers for the corrective action document, piano cost absorption, permanent AM assignment, and commercial concession package.
9 The Linear tracking ticket title or description references the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach and/or Emilia Cruz piano damage.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The Linear ticket description explicitly references both 'Raj Patel — Chain-of-Custody Incident Response' (with details about SentinelChain, IT escort, and warehouse storage) and 'Emilia Cruz — Piano Damage Claim Resolution' (with details about the Steinway scratch).
✓Run #2The trajectory confirms the Linear ticket (OPS-483) description covered both the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach and the Emilia Cruz piano damage as explicit remediation items.
✓Run #3The Linear ticket description explicitly includes Item 1 about Raj Patel's chain-of-custody breach (7 AM crew, 9 AM escort, SentinelChain warehouse) and Item 3 about Emilia Cruz's piano damage ($1,200 rider, $3,500 repair estimate).
✓Run #4The Linear ticket description explicitly references the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach (crew loading before escort, warehouse storage) and the Emilia Cruz piano damage (scratched during stairwell extraction, $3,500 repair, missed callback).
